description_long: This is a military-style helicopter with a streamlined fuselage and a digital camouflage paint scheme in shades of green, brown, tan, and black. The helicopter features a large main rotor with four long, straight blades and a smaller tail rotor mounted on the vertical stabilizer. The cockpit is located at the front, with multiple angular windows providing visibility for the pilots. The landing gear consists of two sturdy skids running parallel beneath the fuselage, designed for stable ground contact. The body is constructed primarily from metal, likely reinforced with composite materials for durability and weight reduction. No visible registration numbers or alphanumeric markings are present on the exterior.
description: A camouflaged military helicopter with four-blade main and tail rotors, angular cockpit windows, and robust landing skids.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, the four-blade main rotor, and the digital camouflage pattern.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the main rotor's cross shape and the narrow profile of the fuselage.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail rotor and the full side profile, similar to view 0 but from the other side.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the angular cockpit windows, nose shape, and the symmetry of the rotor blades.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored dark gray or black, with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small tail rotor with two blades, mounted vertically on the tail fin, colored dark gray or black, with no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel landing skids made of metal, colored in camouflage to match the fuselage, with visible crossbars for structural support.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Four angular cockpit windows at the front, arranged in a wraparound configuration, tinted dark, with thin black framing.
